 {"id":"gt-3x0z.4","title":"Phase 2.1: gt spawn --molecule bonds in ephemeral","description":"Make gt spawn molecule-aware with ephemeral bonding.

## New Flag

```bash
gt spawn --issue gt-xxx --molecule mol-polecat-work
```

## Behavior

1. Create polecat with fresh worktree (existing)
2. Bond molecule in ephemeral: `bd mol bond mol-polecat-work --ephemeral`
3. Link molecule root to source issue
4. Include molecule context in work assignment mail
5. Start session

## Work Assignment Mail

```
Subject: Work Assignment: Fix lifecycle bug [MOLECULE]

You are working on gt-rixa as part of molecule mol-polecat-work.

Molecule instance: eph-abc123
Current step: read-assignment (1/8)

Follow the molecule workflow. When complete, generate summary and squash.
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:33:45.082722-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:25:15.106346-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:25:15.106346-08:00","close_reason":"Already merged to main","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.4","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:33:45.084965-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.4","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:40.385365-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.4","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:40.457259-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-3x0z.5","title":"Phase 2.2: gt prime shows ephemeral molecule context","description":"Update gt prime to detect and display ephemeral molecule state.

## Detection

1. Check for active ephemeral molecule assigned to current identity
2. Parse molecule progress (current step, total steps)
3. Show in prime output

## Output

```
🔧 Polecat furiosa, checking in.

📦 Molecule: mol-polecat-work (eph-abc123)
   Step 3/8: do-work
   Source: gt-rixa
   Started: 10 minutes ago

Run 'bd mol status' for full molecule state.
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/nux","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:33:45.164431-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:34:17.74355-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:34:17.74355-08:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.5","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:33:45.16487-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.5","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T15:22:46.151626-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-3x0z.6","title":"Phase 2.3: Polecat CLAUDE.md molecule workflow protocol","description":"Update polecat prompting for molecule-based work.

## CLAUDE.md Updates

Add section on molecule workflow:

```markdown
## Molecule Workflow

When assigned a molecule (check gt prime output):

1. Follow molecule steps in order
2. Mark steps complete: bd mol step complete <step-id>
3. Before signaling done:
   a. Generate summary of work performed
   b. Run: bd squash <molecule-root> --summary "<your summary>"
4. Then signal done as normal

### Summary Guidelines

Your summary should include:
- What was the task?
- What did you do?
- What was the outcome?
- Any issues or follow-ups?

Keep it to 2-4 sentences. This becomes the permanent record.
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/rictus","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:33:45.24122-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:30:46.776078-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:30:46.776078-08:00","close_reason":"Added molecule workflow section to polecat.md.tmpl with step-by-step protocol and summary guidelines","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.6","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:33:45.241575-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.6","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T15:22:47.355729-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-3x0z.7","title":"Phase 3.1: Summary generation protocol","description":"Define how agents generate summaries for squash.

## The Problem

Beads doesn't make AI calls (inversion of control). Gas Town agents must:
1. Generate their own summary before calling squash
2. Pass summary to bd squash command

## Summary Template

```
Task: <source issue title>
Action: <what was done - fix/implement/refactor/etc>
Outcome: <result - tests pass, committed, needs follow-up, etc>
Details: <1-2 sentences of specifics if needed>
```

## Example

```
Task: Fix lifecycle parser matching bug (gt-rixa)
Action: Reordered conditional checks in parseLifecycleRequest
Outcome: Tests passing, committed to polecat/furiosa
Details: The 'cycle' keyword was matching 'lifecycle:' prefix. Now checks restart/shutdown first, uses word boundary for cycle.
```

## Command

```bash
bd squash eph-abc123 --summary "Task: Fix lifecycle parser..."
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/nux","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:12.86627-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:49:09.904839-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.7","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:12.868178-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.7","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:40.530235-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-3x0z.8","title":"Phase 3.2: mol-polecat-work squash step","description":"Add summary+squash as final steps in mol-polecat-work.

## Current Steps

1. read-assignment
2. understand-task
3. implement-solution
4. verify-tests
5. commit-work
6. signal-done

## Updated Steps

1. read-assignment
2. understand-task
3. implement-solution
4. verify-tests
5. commit-work
6. **generate-summary** ← NEW
7. **squash-molecule** ← NEW
8. signal-done

## Step Definitions

### generate-summary
Agent writes a concise summary following the template.
Save to a local variable or temp file.

### squash-molecule
Run: bd squash $MOLECULE_ROOT --summary "$SUMMARY"
This creates digest in main beads, cleans ephemeral.

## Update Location

This requires updating the mol-polecat-work definition in beads molecule catalog.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:12.946518-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:12.946518-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.8","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:12.946899-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.8","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:40.601367-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-3x0z.9","title":"Phase 4.1: mol-deacon-patrol uses ephemeral","description":"Deacon patrol cycles run as ephemeral molecules.

## Current Deacon Loop

```
while running:
    check_heartbeat()
    check_mail()
    sleep(interval)
```

## Molecule-Based Loop

```
while running:
    mol = bd mol bond mol-deacon-patrol-cycle --ephemeral
    execute_cycle(mol):
        check_heartbeat()
        check_mail()
        log_status()
    summary = generate_cycle_summary()
    bd squash mol --summary summary
    sleep(interval)
```

## Benefits

- Each cycle is tracked
- Digests show daemon health over time
- Can query: 'show me patrol cycles from last hour'
- Crash mid-cycle → ephemeral shows where we were","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:13.026048-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:13.026048-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.9","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:13.026388-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.9","depends_on_id":"gt-3x0z.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:34:40.672531-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x0z.9","depends_on_id":"gt-rana.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T15:20:27.460976-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-3x1","title":"Update Refinery to use Beads merge queue","description":"Replace branch discovery with Beads queue in the Refinery module:

Current (internal/refinery/manager.go):
- Scans for polecat/* branches
- Creates MR objects on-the-fly

New:
- Pull from Beads: bd ready --type=merge-request
- Process each MR
- Close with merge commit: bd close <id> --reason="Merged at <sha>"
- Handle failures: bd update <id> --status=blocked --reason="..."

The Engineer (agent) becomes Beads-native.
The Refinery (module) provides the infrastructure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:37.96436-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T15:24:39.1231-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T14:47:51.65991-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-3x1","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:55.812433-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-3x1","depends_on_id":"gt-svi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:03:12.814463-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-id36.6","title":"Deacon event/timer callbacks: reactive triggers","description":"
Implement event-triggered and timer-based callbacks in the Deacon.

## Timer Callbacks

Agents can schedule future wakes by mailing the Deacon:
```
Subject: TIMER: gastown/witness wake at 2025-12-20T16:00:00Z
Body: Please nudge me - I'm waiting for external dependency
```

Deacon processing:
1. Parse timer mail
2. Check if time has passed
3. If yes: mail the agent "WAKE: Timer fired"
4. Close the timer mail

## Event Subscriptions

Plugins/agents can subscribe to events:
```yaml
# In kernel bead or plugin config
event_triggers:
  - event: "issue.created"
    action: mail
    target: "plugins/work-oracle"
    
  - event: "mr.submitted"
    action: run
    plugin: "review-oracle"
    
  - event: "agent.stuck"
    action: escalate
    target: human
```

## Event Sources

Events come through mail to deacon/:
```
Subject: EVENT: issue.created gt-abc123
Body: New issue created in gastown rig
```

## Event Types

Initial event types:
- `issue.created` - New bead created
- `issue.closed` - Bead closed
- `mr.submitted` - Merge request submitted
- `mr.merged` - Merge request merged
- `agent.stuck` - Agent appears stuck (from health scan)
- `agent.failed` - Agent remediation failed

## Event Processing

During events step:
1. Read event mail from inbox
2. Match against subscriptions
3. Execute actions:
   - `mail`: Send mail to target
   - `run`: Execute plugin inline
   - `spawn`: Spawn subagent for plugin
   - `escalate`: Mail human

## Relation to Mail

Mail IS the event bus. No separate event system.
Events are just specially-formatted mail to deacon/.

 {"id":"gt-iib","title":"Architecture: Decentralized rig structure with per-rig agents","description":"## Decision

Adopt decentralized architecture where each rig contains all its agents (mayor/, witness/, refinery/, polecats/) rather than centralizing mayor clones at town level.

## Town Level Structure

```
~/ai/                              # Town root
├── config/                        # Town config (VISIBLE, not hidden)
│   ├── town.json                  # {"type": "town"}
│   ├── rigs.json                  # Registry of managed rigs
│   └── federation.json            # Wasteland config (future)
│
├── mayor/                         # Mayor's HOME at town level
│   ├── CLAUDE.md
│   ├── mail/inbox.jsonl
│   └── state.json
│
└── <rigs>/                        # Managed projects
```

## Rig Level Structure (e.g., wyvern)

```
wyvern/                            # Rig = clone of project repo
├── .git/info/exclude              # Gas Town adds: polecats/ refinery/ witness/ mayor/
├── .beads/                        # Beads (if project uses it)
├── [project files]                # Clean project code on main
│
├── polecats/                      # Worker clones
│   └── <name>/                    # Each is a git clone
│
├── refinery/
│   ├── rig/                       # Refinery's clone
│   ├── state.json
│   └── mail/inbox.jsonl
│
├── witness/                       # NEW: Per-rig pit boss
│   ├── rig/                       # Witness's clone
│   ├── state.json
│   └── mail/inbox.jsonl
│
└── mayor/
    ├── rig/                       # Mayor's clone for this rig
    └── state.json
```

## Key Decisions

1. **Visible config dir**: `config/` not `.gastown/` (models don't find hidden dirs)
2. **Witness per-rig**: Each rig has its own Witness (pit boss) with its own clone
3. **Mayor decentralized**: Mayor's clones live IN each rig at `<rig>/mayor/rig/`
4. **Minimal invasiveness**: Only `.git/info/exclude` modified, no commits to project
5. **Clone subdir name**: Keep `rig/` for consistency (refinery/rig/, witness/rig/, mayor/rig/)

## Role Detection

- Town root or mayor/ → Mayor (town level)
- Rig root → Mayor (canonical main)
- <rig>/mayor/rig/ → Mayor (rig-specific)
- <rig>/refinery/rig/ → Refinery
- <rig>/witness/rig/ → Witness
- <rig>/polecats/<name>/ → Polecat

## Migration from PGT

- `mayor/rigs/<rig>/` → `<rig>/mayor/rig/`
- `<rig>/town/` → eliminated (rig root IS the clone)
- Add `witness/` to each rig","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:19.913928-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:40.461186-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:40.461186-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-iib","depends_on_id":"gt-u1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:21:40.374551-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-iu23","title":"Polecat doesn't auto-start after spawn inject - requires manual nudge","description":"## Problem

After `gt spawn --issue <id> --create`, the polecat session shows Claude Code started with the injected prompt, but Claude doesn't begin processing. The prompt just sits there until manually nudged.

## Evidence

```
$ gt spawn --issue gt-rixa --rig gastown --create
...
✓ Session started. Attach with: gt session at gastown/furiosa
  Polecat nudged to start working
  Witness notified to monitor startup
```

Session shows:
```
> You have a work assignment. Run 'gt mail inbox' to see it, then start
  working on issue gt-rixa.

  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)
```

But Claude doesn't respond. Manual nudge required:
```
$ gt nudge gt-gastown-furiosa "Please start working..."
```

After nudge, polecat immediately starts working correctly.

## Hypothesis

The spawn inject happens before Claude Code is fully initialized. The text arrives in the input buffer, but Claude hasn't started listening yet. By the time Claude starts, the input has already been 'consumed' as initial prompt text but not submitted.

## Resolution Plan

This will be solved by the **polecat molecule workflow** (mol-polecat-work), which provides structured lifecycle management. The molecule approach handles startup, work, and shutdown as discrete steps with proper state tracking.

**Blocked on**: beads/crew/dave completing ephemeral molecules (bd mol bond, ephemeral beads repo).

## Workaround

For now, use `gt nudge` if a polecat doesn't start within ~30 seconds of spawn.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/rictus","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:06:27.375686-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:25:07.046422-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-iu23","title":"Polecat doesn't auto-start after spawn inject - requires manual nudge","description":"## Problem

After `gt spawn --issue <id> --create`, the polecat session shows Claude Code started with the injected prompt, but Claude doesn't begin processing. The prompt just sits there until manually nudged.

## Evidence

```
$ gt spawn --issue gt-rixa --rig gastown --create
...
✓ Session started. Attach with: gt session at gastown/furiosa
  Polecat nudged to start working
  Witness notified to monitor startup
```

Session shows:
```
> You have a work assignment. Run 'gt mail inbox' to see it, then start
  working on issue gt-rixa.

  ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)
```

But Claude doesn't respond. Manual nudge required:
```
$ gt nudge gt-gastown-furiosa "Please start working..."
```

After nudge, polecat immediately starts working correctly.

## Hypothesis

The spawn inject happens before Claude Code is fully initialized. The text arrives in the input buffer, but Claude hasn't started listening yet. By the time Claude starts, the input has already been 'consumed' as initial prompt text but not submitted.

## Resolution Plan

This will be solved by the **polecat molecule workflow** (mol-polecat-work), which provides structured lifecycle management. The molecule approach handles startup, work, and shutdown as discrete steps with proper state tracking.

**Blocked on**: beads/crew/dave completing ephemeral molecules (bd mol bond, ephemeral beads repo).

## Workaround

For now, use `gt nudge` if a polecat doesn't start within ~30 seconds of spawn.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:06:27.375686-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:30:06.055799-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-qp98","title":"Refactor: Eliminate state.json, use beads assignee field for polecat state","description":"## Problem

state.json in each polecat worktree duplicates data that should be in beads:
- `issue` field duplicates assignment (should be issue.assignee)
- `state` field duplicates status (derivable from issue.status)
- `name/rig/branch/clone_path` are all derivable from filesystem/git

This violates 'Beads as the data plane' (HOP Decision 001).

## Current State

```json
{
  "name": "Angharad",        // Derivable: basename(cwd)
  "rig": "gastown",          // Derivable: parent dir name
  "state": "working",        // Should be: issue.status
  "clone_path": "/path",     // Derivable: cwd
  "branch": "polecat/X",     // Derivable: git branch
  "issue": "gt-xyz",         // Should be: issue.assignee
  "timestamps": "..."        // Should be in beads history
}
```

## Target State

**Polecat identity**: Derived from worktree path
**Polecat assignment**: `issue.assignee = 'gastown/Angharad'`
**Polecat state**: Derived from issue.status (in_progress = working, closed = done)
**Polecat existence**: Worktree directory exists

## Benefits

1. Single source of truth (no sync issues)
2. Queryable: `bd list --assignee=gastown/Angharad`
3. History tracking via beads
4. Cross-agent coordination via beads sync
5. Simpler code (no state file management)

## Implementation

1. Update `gt spawn` to set issue.assignee instead of writing state.json
2. Update polecat list/status commands to query beads
3. Update Witness to query beads for polecat state
4. Remove state.json read/write code
5. Keep state.json as optional bootstrap cache (perf optimization only)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T11:40:33.389689-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:07:43.519059-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:07:43.519059-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-rana","title":"Patrol System: Agent lifecycle loops with attachments","description":"Enable Gas Town agents to run continuous patrols, survive crashes, and hand off work across sessions.

## Core Concepts
- **Attachments**: Molecules bound to agent's pinned bead until complete
- **Patrols**: Cyclic molecules that loop (deacon, witness, refinery)
- **Quiescent**: Agents that sleep until triggered (witness, refinery)

## Design Doc
See docs/patrol-system-design.md

## Phases
Phase 1: Foundation (attachment field, daemon detection, mol-deacon-patrol)
Phase 2: Quiescent Agents (wake triggers, witness/refinery patrols)
Phase 3: Callbacks and Plugins (mail protocol, plugin runner)
Phase 4: Polish (gt patrol status, metrics, tuning)

## Key Decisions
- Attachment as field on pinned bead (not edge type, for now)
- Mail-based orchestration for all callbacks
- Queue replacement for heartbeat delivery (not pile-up)
- Burn and respawn for patrol loops (not in-place reset)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:23.416949-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:23.416949-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-rana.1","title":"Phase 1.1: Attachment field on pinned beads","description":"Add attached_molecule field to pinned bead schema.

## Schema Change
Add to handoff/pinned bead issues:
- attached_molecule: string (root issue ID of attached mol)
- attached_at: timestamp

## Implementation
- Update beads Issue struct if needed
- Or: use labels/metadata field
- Ensure bd show displays attachment info

## Acceptance
- Can set/clear attachment on a bead
- bd show displays attachment status","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:34.251531-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:51:01.902014-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:51:01.902014-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented attachment fields: AttachMolecule, DetachMolecule, GetAttachment APIs","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rana.1","depends_on_id":"gt-rana","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:34.253378-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

- Daemon knows Deacon's pinned bead ID
- Every heartbeat cycle: bd show <pinned> --json
- Parse attached_molecule field
- If null/empty: Deacon is naked

## Actions on Naked
- Spawn mol-deacon-patrol
- Attach to Deacon's pinned
- Nudge Deacon to start

## Failsafes
- Keepalive file monitoring
- Stale detection (>10min no progress)
- Force nudge on stale

 {"id":"gt-rana.3","title":"Phase 1.3: mol-deacon-patrol definition","description":"Define the Deacon patrol molecule in builtin_molecules.go.

## Steps
1. inbox-check - Handle callbacks from agents
2. health-scan - Ping Witnesses and Refineries
3. plugin-run - Execute registered plugins
4. orphan-check - Find abandoned work
5. session-gc - Clean dead sessions
6. context-check - Check own context limit
7. loop-or-exit - Burn and let daemon respawn, or exit if context high

## Implementation
- Add DeaconPatrolMolecule() to builtin_molecules.go
- Add to BuiltinMolecules() list
- Test with bd mol show mol-deacon-patrol","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/dementus","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:54.555938-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:39:16.769062-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:39:16.769062-08:00","close_reason":"Closed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-rana.3","depends_on_id":"gt-rana","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:54.55744-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-rana.4","title":"Phase 1.4: Basic patrol runner in Deacon","description":"Deacon CLAUDE.md and prime context for patrol execution.

## Deacon Context
- Update Deacon CLAUDE.md with patrol instructions
- On wake: check pinned bead for attachment
- If attached: resume molecule from current step
- Execute steps, close each when done
- On final step: burn molecule, go naked

## gt prime Enhancement
- Detect if agent has attached molecule
- Show molecule progress in prime output
- Include patrol-specific instructions

 {"id":"gt-vc1n","title":"Tmux status line: rig color themes and worker identity display","description":"## Summary

Customize tmux status line for Gas Town workers with:
1. Per-rig configurable color themes
2. Clear worker name and role visibility

## Current Problem

- Only mayor shows in status line (and truncated)
- Can't tell which rig/worker you're looking at
- All sessions look the same

## Proposed Design

### Per-rig colors
```yaml
# In rig config or beads
theme:
  primary: '#ff6600'    # Orange for gastown
  secondary: '#333333'
  accent: '#ffcc00'
```

### Status line format
```
[gastown/Rictus] polecat | gt-70b3 | branch: polecat/Rictus
[beads/emma] crew | working | branch: main  
[mayor] coordinator | idle
```

### Components
- Rig name with rig color
- Worker name
- Role (polecat/crew/mayor/witness/refinery)
- Current issue or status
- Branch name

## Configuration

Could use pinned beads for this (see gm-w13, beads-6v2):
- `bd show <rig>-theme` returns theme config
- Stored as pinned bead, always available
- Part of 'config in beads data plane' initiative","notes":"Implementation complete. Core features: per-rig color themes, dynamic status line with issue/mail indicators, gt theme/issue commands.  {"id":"gt-vci","title":"Mayor handoff mail template","description":"Add MAYOR_HANDOFF mail template to templates.py.

## Template Function

def mayor_handoff(
    active_swarms: List[SwarmStatus],
    rig_status: Dict[str, RigStatus],
    pending_escalations: List[Escalation],
    in_flight_decisions: List[Decision],
    recent_actions: List[str],
    delegated_work: List[DelegatedItem],
    user_requests: List[str],
    next_steps: List[str],
    warnings: Optional[str] = None,
    session_duration: Optional[str] = None,
) -> Message:
    metadata = {
        'template': 'MAYOR_HANDOFF',
        'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        'session_duration': session_duration,
        'active_swarm_count': len(active_swarms),
        'pending_escalation_count': len(pending_escalations),
    }
    # ... format sections ...
    return Message.create(
        sender='mayor/',
        recipient='mayor/',
        subject='Session Handoff',
        body=body,
        priority='high',
    )

## Metadata Fields

- template: MAYOR_HANDOFF
- timestamp: ISO format
- session_duration: Human readable
- active_swarm_count: Number of active swarms
- pending_escalation_count: Number of escalations

## Mail Priority

Use priority='high' to ensure handoff is seen on startup.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:30.26323-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:59.550689-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-vci","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.554108-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

- Confusing role information

## Fix

 {"id":"gt-vjw","title":"Swarm learning: Session cleanup missing from swarm workflow","description":"## Problem

After Enders Game swarm completed (18 issues merged), 16 polecat sessions were left running but idle. No automated cleanup occurred.

## What Should Happen

1. Witness detects polecat completed work (idle at prompt)
2. Witness verifies git state is clean
3. Witness shuts down session
4. Witness reports completion to Mayor

## GGT Components

- gt-cxx: Witness context cycling (covers self-cycling)
- gt-u1j.9: Witness daemon heartbeat loop
- gt-kmn.6: Witness swarm landing protocol

## Recommendation

Add to Witness responsibilities:
- Monitor for 'work complete' signals (DONE keyword, idle detection)
- Automated session shutdown after verification
- Swarm completion reporting to Mayor

See also: architecture.md 'Worker Cleanup (Witness-Owned)' section which describes this but it wasn't implemented in PGT.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:27:52.796587-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:37.409965-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:37.409965-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-vnp9","title":"tmux notifications: display-message too subtle, use send-keys instead","description":"## Problem

`tmux display-message` notifications are not visible enough - they appear briefly in the status bar and are easy to miss.

## Current Behavior

router.go uses:
```go
r.tmux.DisplayMessageDefault(sessionID, notification)
```

## What Works

Sending echo commands directly to the terminal:
```bash
tmux send-keys -t <session> "echo '📬 NEW MAIL from mayor'" Enter
```

## Proposed Fix

Change notification method to send visible output to the terminal, perhaps with a box/banner:
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
📬 NEW MAIL from mayor
Subject: <subject>
Run: bd mail inbox
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

## Considerations

- This interrupts the terminal output (acceptable for important mail)
- Could check if Claude is mid-response and queue notification
- Or use tmux popup if available","notes":"Additional issues:
1. Enter key not sent properly when chained with send-keys
2. Need to debounce and send Enter separately
3. Correct pattern: send text, sleep briefly, then send Enter","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:35:28.542985-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:00:29.342381-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:00:29.342381-08:00"}
